critic_raw: The design handles most standard LTR pitfalls well. I want to focus on one nuanced issue: the design uses 'query frequency quintile' and 'seller quality quintile' for stratification, but these quintiles are computed on the full 18-month dataset. If they are computed on training data only, this is fine. But if they are computed on the full dataset (including test), then items in the test set are stratified using information from the future (their quintile placement may shift as more data comes in). Additionally, the embedding model is described as 'fit once on the training set' — but if this involves fine-tuning on training data, the embedding vocabulary and representations may not generalize to new items introduced in months 16-18.
defender_raw: Both concerns are addressed. The quintile assignment for stratification uses query frequency computed from historical training data — the design states 'query frequency decile (indicating head vs. tail queries)' as a query context feature, which must be fit on training data (as stated: 'All scaling, embedding models, and transformations are fit on the training set'). Applying training-set quintile thresholds to validation and test queries is standard practice. On embedding generalization: frozen pretrained embeddings are applied to new items as they appear — new items in months 16-18 receive embeddings from the frozen encoder applied to their titles and descriptions, which is exactly how a deployed system would handle new catalog items. This is a strength of neural embeddings over pure BM25 — they can represent semantically similar items that share no exact keyword overlap.
adjudication_raw: Two concerns raised: (1) quintile stratification computed on full dataset, (2) embedding generalization to new items. Defender correctly clarified both: preprocessing and quintile thresholds are fit on training data per the design's stated fit-apply separation; frozen embeddings handle new items via the pre-trained encoder's generalization. Both concerns are resolved. Verdict: defense_wins.
